steps.title: Identify the relevant rule and classify Tongass
steps.content:
The passage states two rules: (1) in large forests, the model's predictions for large-mammal populations are too high; (2) in small forests, its predictions for small-mammal populations are too high. Tongass is explicitly described as a large forest. Rule (1) therefore applies: the model will over-predict large-mammal populations there.
  • Choice A — exaggerate the effect of increasing human activity on small mammals.: Applies the small-forest rule (small mammals too high) to a large forest — wrong category. ✗
  • Choice B — overestimate the actual population sizes of large mammals.: Directly follows from the large-forest rule: predictions for large mammals are too high, meaning the model overestimates those populations. ✓
  • Choice C — ignore the predator-prey relationships between large and small mammals.: Predator-prey relationships are not mentioned in the passage; this introduces an outside concept. ✗
  • Choice D — reflect factors affecting large mammals other than increasing human activity.: The passage discusses only population-size prediction errors, not what factors the model reflects. ✗

steps.title: Apply the rule to reach a conclusion
steps.content: Tongass is a large forest, so the large-forest rule applies: the model predicts large-mammal populations that are too high, meaning it overestimates actual large-mammal population sizes. That is the correct answer.
